title: Asclepius — changelog
description: Cryptographically chained release history for the Asclepius clinical-AI trust substrate. Each entry references the previous hash; the changelog is itself a Merkle log.

items.title: v0.6.0 — India profile · ABDM artefact, family graph, DPDP break-glass.
items.summary: Three vendor-neutral consent-side primitives (ConsentArtefact, FamilyGraph, EmergencyOverride) plus six new Purpose values aligned with ABDM. ADR-013 + ADR-014. End-to-end demo at examples/07_india_profile/.
items.content_text: Round 90. Three vendor-neutral consent-side primitives — ConsentArtefact (ABDM-shaped wire record), FamilyGraph (proxy-for-subject relation graph), EmergencyOverride (DPDP § 7 break-glass with mandatory backfill window) — plus six new Purpose values (prescription_resolution, second_opinion, specialist_referral, billing_audit, longitudinal_outcomes_research, emergency_clinical_access). ADR-013 documents the primitives; ADR-014 codifies enum-extension as a governance event. Same-name disambiguation (ADR-012) shipped: ~80 GitHub repos share the name; we are the substrate, not the model. Examples 05 (Microsoft Asclepius wrap), 06 (starmpcc/Asclepius wrap), 07 (India profile in motion). L2-Medical conformance scaffold with 4/128 doctest assertions against the ACL 2025 Asclepius-Med benchmark. Kernel test suite: 1388 cases / 35086 assertions / 0 failed.

items.title: v0.5.0 — Round 89: reverse-hash lookup, tenant histograms, lifecycle summaries.
items.summary: Reverse-hash lookup, tenant histograms, balanced-shape probe; lifecycle summaries, atomic attest, consent metric counters. No on-disk format changes.
items.content_text: Round 89 (parts one and two). find_inference_by_input_hash() reverse index. tenant_event_counts() and tenant body-size histograms. balanced-shape probe. Consent: token_lifecycle_summary, tokens_revoked_for_patient, patients_with_active_count, has_pending_expiry, compact_state. Inference: commit_then_attest, commit_with_metadata, is_pending. Runtime: flush_consent_to_metrics, current_seq.

items.title: v0.1.0 — The substrate, open-sourced.
items.summary: First public release. C++20 runtime with signed Merkle ledger, policy chain, drift detection, consent registry, and offline-verifiable evidence bundles. Apache 2.0.
items.content_text: First public release of the Asclepius runtime. Apache 2.0. C++20 with Runtime, Inference, Ledger, PolicyChain, DriftMonitor, ConsentRegistry, EvaluationHarness. Append-only Merkle ledger over SQLite WAL. BLAKE2b-256 + Ed25519. Policies: phi_scrubber, schema_validator, clinical_action_filter, length_limit. USTAR evidence bundles, signed manifest, offline-verifiable. Python bindings, CLI, three worked examples. 52 doctest cases / 687 assertions, all green.

items.title: v0.0.9 — Conformance: L1 audit suite green.
items.summary: Pinned the on-disk ledger format (spec.md v1) and ran the L1 conformance suite end-to-end.
items.content_text: Pinned the on-disk ledger format. Sign-input layout fixed. Drift severity bands tuned against published clinical-AI deployment data.

items.title: v0.0.7 — Evidence bundles ship.
items.summary: USTAR tar emit + offline-verify. Manifest signed by the ledger key.

items.title: v0.0.5 — Drift on the ground floor.
items.summary: DriftMonitor with PSI / KS / EMD on per-feature histograms.

items.title: v0.0.3 — Policy chain composed.
items.summary: First version of the PolicyChain with allow/modify/block verdicts.

items.title: v0.0.1 — Genesis. The Merkle ledger compiles.
items.summary: First commit. Append-only signed log over SQLite. verify() walks the chain.
